title: Simple Bookstore API
statement: Implement a REST API for managing a small bookstore. The API should allow users to create, read, update and delete books. Books have a unique ID, title, author and publication year. Assume that all books are stored in memory for simplicity. Implement the following endpoints: GET /books, POST /books, GET /books/:id, PUT /books/:id, DELETE /books/:id.
constraints: Must use only the in-memory array provided
Use Express.js as the web framework

endpoints.description: Returns a list of all books in the bookstore. Success response should include a list of book objects.

Creates a new book in the bookstore. The request body should contain the book's title, author and publication year. Success response should include the newly created book object and a 201 status code.
Returns a single book by its ID. Success response should include the book object.
Updates an existing book in the bookstore. The request body should contain the updated book details. Success response should include the updated book object.
Deletes a book from the bookstore. Success response should indicate the deletion was successful.
